§ Enjoying

Enjoying

Books, records, films, podcasts. The things I keep coming back to, lightly annotated.

  • § Movie · Apr 12 2026

    Perfect Days

    dir. Wim Wenders

    A film about attention, stubbornly refusing to be a film about work. Re-watch; better the second time. I cried at a tree.
  • § Movie · Apr 05 2026

    Anatomy of a Fall

    dir. Justine Triet

    The best film about language I have seen in years. Every witness is also translating.
  • § Movie · Mar 28 2026

    Past Lives

    dir. Celine Song

    I do not know how to write about this one yet. Took a long walk after.
  • § Movie · Mar 20 2026

    The Zone of Interest

    dir. Jonathan Glazer

    Sound design as moral architecture. The hardest film I have watched in a decade. I will not rewatch it soon.
  • § Movie · Mar 14 2026

    Paddington 2

    dir. Paul King

    Medicinal. Nothing else to add.
  • § Movie · Mar 02 2026

    La Chimera

    dir. Alice Rohrwacher

    Rohrwacher keeps making films I want to live inside. A quiet one, but the kind where the light is doing all the work.
  • § Movie · Feb 22 2026

    Paris, Texas

    dir. Wim Wenders

    Revisited after Perfect Days. The long booth scene is still the best scene of dialogue in cinema, for my money.
  • § Movie · Feb 10 2026

    All of Us Strangers

    dir. Andrew Haigh

    A ghost story that is really a story about carrying. Held up better a week later than it did the night of.
  • § Movie · Jan 30 2026

    Nope

    dir. Jordan Peele

    Rewatched on 35mm at the Roxie. The cinema is a character; that is the whole thesis.
  • § Movie · Jan 18 2026

    Portrait of a Lady on Fire

    dir. Celine Sciamma

    Annual rewatch. The page-28 scene, always.
  • § Music · 2025

    Census Designated

    Jane Remover

    Lives on in the car. The kind of record where production choices keep rearranging themselves on the 20th listen. I keep finding small rooms inside it.
  • § Music · 2024

    Bright Future

    Adrianne Lenker

    Recorded live to tape in a cabin, and you can hear the walls. The kind of album that does not help you feel better, just feel it on purpose.
Page 1 of 4
